REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF HIDEKI ITO'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE "NO DAMAGES" AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE "FAILURE TO STATE A CLAIM" DEFENSE AS THIS IS NOT AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND ON ALL OTHER GROUNDS FOR DEFENDANT TO STATE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AS TO ALLEGE SPECIFICALLY "ANY INTERESTS THE DEFENDANT HAS OR CLAIMS IN THE PROPERTY." DEFENDANT SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF HIDEKI ITO Amended DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege (1) as to the first cause of action facts in support of physical or mental harm caused by "other treatment" within the meaning of WIC 15610.07; and (2) as to the second cause of action facts in support of undue influence to support a claim for elder abuse within the meaning of WIC 15610.07 ("undue influence" is referenced in the opposition, but is not mentioned in the second cause of action of the operative cross-complaint).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF HIDEKI ITO'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the "no damages" affirmative defense. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the "failure to state a claim" defense as this is not an affirmative defense. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on all other grounds for Defendant to state facts in support of each affirmative defense as to allege specifically "any interests the defendant has or claims in the property." Defendant shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended answer.

Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not reported (D501)
